name: La Serra de l'Aixorta — Trail run circular
region_intro: Jogging around Castell De Castells offers an opportunity to explore a region characterized by rugged limestone mountains, deep valleys, and varied terrain. The area features a network of trails, including peaceful forest paths, stone terraces, and wider countryside farm roads, often winding through terraced almond and olive groves. This diverse landscape provides a range of running experiences, from gentle routes to more challenging mountain trails. Best jogging routes around Castell De Castells The most popular jogging route is Running circular at Embalse de Guadalest, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Guadalest Reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is La Serra de l'Aixorta — Trail run circular, a difficult 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Mallada del Llop – Morro dels Regalls loop from Fageca, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail leading through varied mountain landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Jogging around Castell De Castells is defined by mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and terraced almond and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult mountain running trails. The routes in Castell De Castells are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Castell De Castells's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Castell de Castells?
A: There are over 60 running routes around Castell de Castells, offering a wide range of experiences for different fitness levels. These include everything from moderate paths to challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Castell de Castells?
A: The running trails in Castell de Castells feature diverse terrain, from peaceful forest paths and stone terraces to wider countryside farm roads. You'll encounter rugged limestone mountains, rocky and gravel surfaces, and often run through picturesque almond and olive groves, providing a varied and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in the area?
A: While many routes in Castell de Castells are rated moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain, there are options for less experienced runners. Look for routes that stick to wider farm roads or gentler paths through the valleys. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872638619/running-circular-at-embalse-de-guadalest" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Embalse de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that offers beautiful views without extreme elevation.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see while running?
A: Castell de Castells offers spectacular natural beauty. You can enjoy panoramic views of deep valleys and the Guadalest Reservoir from elevated positions. Don't miss the Natural Spring – Font de la Retura, a refreshing stop, or the impressive Natural Arches (Els Arcs) located just outside the village, which are among the largest in Europe.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region blends natural beauty with rich history. You can explore the Prehistoric Rock Art at Pla de Petracos, a UNESCO-protected site with Neolithic rock art. The remains of the Moorish Watchtower (Penya Castellet) on a hilltop also offer historical context and commanding views, accessible via a challenging footpath.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Castell de Castells?
A: The running routes in Castell de Castells are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Runners frequently praise the stunning mountainous vistas, the tranquility of the trails, and the diverse landscapes that include almond and olive groves. The well-marked paths also contribute to a positive experience.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Castell de Castells are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872574229/la-serra-de-l-aixorta-trail-run-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serra de l'Aixorta — Trail run circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging route through mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the Guadalest Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The Guadalest Reservoir is a prominent feature in the area and several routes offer views of or run alongside it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872638619/running-circular-at-embalse-de-guadalest" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Embalse de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing excellent reservoir views.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Castell de Castells?
A: The region is generally pleasant for running outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn offer ideal conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, including blooming almond trees in late winter/early spring. Winter can also be suitable, though some higher mountain paths might be cooler.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges to see near the running trails?
A: Yes, the area boasts impressive natural formations. While not directly on every running path, you can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4155453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular attractions in the wider region and can be combined with a run or visited separately.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Castell de Castells?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Castell de Castells, particularly in the village center or at common starting points for popular trails. Given the rural nature of the area, finding a spot is usually manageable, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.
tips: Els Arcs are the arch formations that have been produced due to the erosion of the limestone rock. This natural monument is located in the northern part of the Serra de la Aixortá.

If you want adventure and have the best views of the Guadalest valley, do not hesitate to climb to the top of Penya Alta. At 1220 meters above sea level, the summit offers wonderful views and at the same time a challenge to reach it. You can take different alternative paths with different degrees of difficulty but all very entertaining and attractive.
